owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

claim(uint256,bytes32).notice: Claim the escrow payout and distribute to quorum members.

leaderShareBps().notice: Payout split: leader share in basis points (6000 = 60%)

recoverToken(address,address).notice: Recover any tokens accidentally sent to this contract.

setSplit(uint256,uint256).notice: Update payout split. Total must equal 10000 bps.

verifierShareBps().notice: Payout split: each verifier share in basis points (2000 = 20%)

@notice Payout split: each verifier share in basis points (2000 = 20%)

typeDescriptions.typeString: function (bytes32) view external returns (struct IResultRegistry.InferenceResult memory)

documentation.text: @notice Update payout split. Total must equal 10000 bps.
@notice Recover any tokens accidentally sent to this contract.

@title PayoutClaimer
 @notice Acts as the escrow beneficiary — claims settled funds and distributes
         payments to the inference quorum (leader + verifiers).
         Flow:
           1. User creates Reineira escrow with `owner = address(this)` and
              `resolver = InferenceGate`.
           2. After the ICL marks the job as verified in ResultRegistry,
              anyone calls `claim(escrowId)`.
           3. This contract calls `ConfidentialEscrow.redeem(escrowId)`.
           4. Reineira internally checks `InferenceGate.isConditionMet()` →
              reads `ResultRegistry.isConditionMet()` → returns true.
           5. Escrow releases cUSDC to this contract.
           6. cUSDC is distributed to the leader and verifiers.
